### Fix 2 Analysis (Line 28 - Icon)

- `Icon` is imported from `@patternfly/react-core` (line 28)

The `Icon` component itself is a different component (`@patternfly/react-core/components/Icon`). The incident matched on the import name `Icon` but this is likely a false match ‚ÄĒ the `Icon` component in this file is the standalone `Icon` wrapper component (used with `status="danger"` and `status="success"`), not `EmptyStateIcon`.

However, checking PF v6 ‚ÄĒ the `Icon` component was indeed removed/deprecated in v6. Let me look at this more carefully. In PF v6, the `Icon` component still exists in `@patternfly/react-core`. The icons themselves (`CheckCircleIcon`, etc.) can be used directly with `status` props via the icon system.

Actually, looking at the PF v6 migration, the standalone `Icon` component wrapper was removed. Icons can take `color` or be wrapped differently. But the rule text says this is about `EmptyStateIconProps` ‚Üí `EmptyStateProps`, which doesn't match the usage here (plain `Icon` wrapper, not `EmptyStateIcon`).

Given the incident targets `Icon` import specifically, and the fix strategy says `LlmAssisted` from `EmptyStateIconProps` to `EmptyStateProps`, this seems like a mismatched rule. The `Icon` component used here wraps icons with a `status` prop ‚ÄĒ this is unrelated to `EmptyStateIcon`.
